title: Claude Code for Business Automations
source: Article: Claude Code evolved from coding assistant into autonomous business agent. Computer Use = visual screen navigation (fragile, breaks on layout change). MCP = structured read/write connections across SaaS apps (durable). Use cases: Finance (invoice processing, compliance), Sales/CRM (data entry, lead research, LinkedIn outreach), Marketing (ad management, campaign gen, price monitoring), HR (job postings, onboarding), MCP orchestration (cross-platform reporting, pipeline reviews, client onboarding).

scenes.voiceText: Claude Code doesn't just write code. It runs your operations.
scenes.headline: Claude Code runs your *operations*

scenes.voiceText: Most teams still handle this manually. Finance pulls invoice data by hand. Sales copies leads into CRM. Marketing adjusts ad spend one row at a time.
scenes.headline: Still doing this *manually*?

scenes.voiceText: I started wiring Claude Code into real business workflows. It reads invoices, updates records, adjusts campaigns, posts jobs across multiple boards.
scenes.headline: One agent, *all* your tools

scenes.voiceText: Finance, sales, marketing, HR. Five business functions automated. Running 24/7. Starting from just one task.
scenes.headline: What gets automated

scenes.voiceText: The question isn't whether to automate. It's which task you start with.
scenes.headline: Which task do you *start* with?

scenes.voiceText: Pick the most repetitive thing on your team. Start there. Follow for more.
scenes.cta: What's the first task you'd hand to an agent?
